req.title: Manager creates timesheet
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a timesheet through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a UI form submission by a manager to create a timesheet. I selected five tests: (1) EP positive – valid manager submits a fully-populated form and receives 200/201 plus an audit record; (2) NEG – missing mandatory field triggers a 400 validation error on the UI; (3) BVA – notes/description field submitted at exactly the maximum allowed length (boundary character count); (4) NEG/STC – unauthenticated user is redirected or receives 401; (5) DT – forbidden role (employee, not manager) receives 403. Together these cover the happy path, both error pathways specified, and the boundary condition on a text field.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_timesheet_valid_form
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
No duplicate timesheet exists for the same week
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Capture HTTP response status and returned entity ID
Query audit log for the new timesheet ID
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity ID
Timesheet record appears in the list view
Audit log contains one entry referencing the new entity ID
trace: R-WEB-017: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_creates_timesheet_valid_form(manager_page, api_client, valid_timesheet_payload, audit_log_query):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")

    <fill_timesheet_form>(page, valid_timesheet_payload)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/timesheets" in r.url and r.request.method in ("POST",)) as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("timesheetId")
    assert entity_id, "Response must include entity ID"

    expect(page.get_by_test_id("timesheet-success-banner")).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record must exist for new timesheet"
    assert audit_entries[0]["action"] == "CREATE"

tests.name: test_manager_create_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
actions: Observe UI validation message and HTTP response
expected: UI displays a field-level validation error for 'week_ending_date'
No audit log entry is written
trace: R-WEB-017: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_create_timesheet_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(manager_page, incomplete_timesheet_payload, audit_log_query):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")

    payload = {k: v for k, v in incomplete_timesheet_payload.items() if k != "week_ending_date"}
    <fill_timesheet_form>(page, payload)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/timesheets" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    error_locator = page.get_by_test_id("field-error-week_ending_date")
    expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()

    body = response.json()
    assert "week_ending_date" in str(body), "Error body must reference the missing field"

    entries = audit_log_query(entity_id=None, action="CREATE", recent_seconds=5)
    assert entries == [], "No audit entry should be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_manager_create_timesheet_notes_at_max_length
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Maximum allowed length for 'notes' field is known (e.g. 500 characters)
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Set 'notes' field to exactly MAX_NOTES_LENGTH characters
expected: Timesheet is created with the full-length notes value intact
trace: R-WEB-017: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_NOTES_LENGTH = 500

def test_manager_create_timesheet_notes_at_max_length(manager_page, valid_timesheet_payload, audit_log_query):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")

    payload = dict(valid_timesheet_payload)
    payload["notes"] = "A" * MAX_NOTES_LENGTH
    <fill_timesheet_form>(page, payload)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/timesheets" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Max-length notes should succeed; got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("timesheetId")
    assert entity_id, "Entity ID required in response"

    saved = <fetch_timesheet>(entity_id)
    assert len(saved["notes"]) == MAX_NOTES_LENGTH, "Saved notes length must equal MAX_NOTES_LENGTH"

    audit_entries = audit_log_query(entity_id)
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_timesheet
actions: Attempt to navigate to /timesheets without logging in
expected: User is redirected to login page OR receives HTTP 401
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_timesheet(fresh_page, base_url, audit_log_query):
    page = fresh_page
    response = page.goto(f"{base_url}/timesheets")

    is_redirected_to_login = "/login" in page.url or "/signin" in page.url
    is_401 = response is not None and response.status == 401

    assert is_redirected_to_login or is_401, (
        f"Expected redirect to login or 401; got URL={page.url}, status={getattr(response, 'status', 'N/A')}"
    )

    form_locator = page.get_by_test_id("timesheet-form")
    expect(form_locator).not_to_be_visible()

    entries = audit_log_query(entity_id=None, action="CREATE", recent_seconds=5)
    assert entries == [], "No audit entry should exist for unauthenticated attempt"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_employee_cannot_create_timesheet
actions: Attempt to POST to /timesheets with valid timesheet data
Observe HTTP response status and UI behaviour
expected: UI displays an authorisation error or access-denied message
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_forbidden_role_employee_cannot_create_timesheet(employee_page, valid_timesheet_payload, audit_log_query):
    page = employee_page
    page.goto("/timesheets")

    <fill_timesheet_form>(page, valid_timesheet_payload)

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/timesheets" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.get_by_role("button", name="Submit").click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 403, f"Employee role must receive 403; got {response.status}"

    access_denied = page.get_by_test_id("access-denied-message")
    expect(access_denied).to_be_visible()

    entries = audit_log_query(entity_id=None, action="CREATE", recent_seconds=5)
    assert entries == [], "No audit entry should be written for forbidden role attempt"
